title: Water Fill Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher did not fill with water within the expected time.
fullDescription: The E1 error code on Midea dishwashers indicates the machine did not detect sufficient water within approximately 10 minutes of starting the fill cycle. This is typically caused by closed water supply valves, clogged inlet filters, low water pressure, or a faulty water inlet valve.

causes.cause: Water supply valve closed or partially shut

symptoms: E1 displayed on the control panel
No water enters the dishwasher tub
Dishwasher beeps and stops shortly after starting
Very slow water filling before timeout
Dishes come out dirty because wash cycle did not complete

fixSteps.title: Check the water supply valve
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water supply valve under the kitchen sink that feeds the dishwasher. Ensure it is fully open by turning it counterclockwise.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet filter screen
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the inlet hose from the dishwasher. Remove the mesh filter screen and rinse under running water. Reinstall and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Check for hose kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the water supply hose for kinks, sharp bends, or compression. Straighten as needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the inlet valve behind the bottom panel. Disconnect the wire harness and test the solenoid with a multimeter. Normal resistance is 500 to 1500 ohms.

whenToCallPro: If the water inlet valve has failed or household water pressure is consistently low.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E1 error water fill fix

faq.q: Can I reset E1 by turning the dishwasher off and on?
faq.a: You can try a reset, but E1 will return if the water supply issue is not resolved.

faq.q: Why does E1 appear only sometimes?
faq.a: Intermittent E1 can indicate a partially clogged filter or fluctuating water pressure that sometimes falls below the required minimum.

faq.q: Is E1 the same as a no-water error?
faq.a: Yes. E1 specifically means the dishwasher could not detect adequate water within the fill timeout period.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot drain water properly.
fullDescription: The E2 error code indicates the dishwasher failed to drain water within the allowed time. Water remains in the tub after the drain cycle completes. This is commonly caused by a clogged drain filter, blocked drain hose, or a failed drain pump.

causes.cause: Clogged drain filter with food debris
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher
E2 displayed on the control panel
Dishes remain dirty from incomplete cycle
Foul odor from stagnant water
Drain pump runs but water does not exit

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack. Twist and lift out the drain filter assembly from the bottom of the tub. Rinse under running water and scrub with a soft brush to remove all food debris.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out and inspect the drain hose. Ensure it is not kinked and has a proper high loop under the counter to prevent backflow. Disconnect and check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check garbage disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a garbage disposal, verify the knockout plug inside the disposal inlet has been removed. A blocked inlet prevents drainage.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the drain pump underneath. Disconnect the pump motor wires and test with a multimeter. Replace if no continuity.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed or there is a plumbing blockage beyond the dishwasher.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E2 error drain fix

faq.q: How do I manually drain the dishwasher?
faq.a: Use towels and a cup to scoop standing water. Then clean the drain filter and check the drain hose.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain filter after every few cycles, or at least weekly with regular use.

faq.q: Can food scraps cause E2?
faq.a: Yes. Large food particles can clog the filter and drain pump. Always scrape plates before loading.

title: Heating Element Error
shortDescription: The water heating element is not reaching the target temperature.
fullDescription: The E3 error code indicates the dishwasher heater element failed to heat the water to the required temperature within the expected time. This can result from a failed heater, faulty temperature sensor, or wiring issues.

causes.cause: Loose wiring to the heater element

symptoms: E3 displayed during wash cycle
Water remains cold throughout the cycle
Dishes come out greasy and not properly cleaned
Cycle runs much longer than normal before error
Detergent tablet does not dissolve completely

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the heater element connections underneath. Disconnect the wires and measure resistance with a multimeter. Normal reading is 15 to 30 ohms. Infinite resistance means the element is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor near the heater. Disconnect and test resistance. At room temperature expect 10K to 50K ohms. Replace if abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all wiring between the heater, sensor, and control board for loose or corroded connections. Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the element has failed, drain the dishwasher, remove the old element from the bottom of the tub, and install the replacement. Ensure the gasket seats properly to prevent leaks.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable working with electrical components around water.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E3 error heating element fix

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without heating?
faq.a: Some cycles may complete without heating, but cleaning will be poor. Grease and baked-on food require hot water.

faq.q: Will connecting to a hot water line help?
faq.a: Connecting to a hot water supply can partially compensate, but the dishwasher still needs its heater for sanitize cycles and to maintain temperature during long washes.

faq.q: How long should the heater take?
faq.a: The heater typically raises water temperature by 15 to 25 degrees within 10 to 15 minutes. E3 triggers after this time without reaching the target.

title: Overflow Protection Error
shortDescription: The anti-flood sensor has detected water in the base of the dishwasher.
fullDescription: The E4 error code indicates the overflow protection float sensor has detected water accumulation in the base pan of the dishwasher. This safety feature stops all water operations to prevent flooding. A leak from the tub, door seal, or internal hoses is the typical cause.
symptoms: E4 displayed and dishwasher stops
Water pooling under the dishwasher
Dishwasher beeps continuously
Water visible in the base pan area
E4 appears even with empty dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply and pull the dishwasher out. Look underneath for water. Check the door seal, hose connections, and drain pump area.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door gasket around the entire perimeter. Look for tears, gaps, or debris preventing a proper seal. Clean the gasket and door edge with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the dishwasher slightly to access the base pan. Use towels to absorb all water. Check that the float switch drops back to its resting position.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the bottom panel and inspect all internal hose connections. Tighten clamps and check for cracked or damaged hoses.

whenToCallPro: If the tub is cracked or leaks persist after replacing the door seal.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E4 error overflow leak fix

faq.q: Is E4 a flood risk?
faq.a: E4 is the dishwasher protecting against flooding. Address the leak promptly to prevent water damage.

faq.q: Can using too much detergent cause E4?
faq.a: Excessive suds can push water out of the tub through seals, triggering the overflow sensor. Use the recommended detergent amount.

faq.q: Why does E4 appear with an empty dishwasher?
faq.a: If the float switch is stuck in the up position from a previous leak, it will trigger E4 even when dry. Free the stuck float or replace it.

title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level sensor is not reading correctly.
fullDescription: The E5 error code indicates the dishwasher water level sensor is returning abnormal readings. The control board cannot determine the correct water level in the tub, preventing proper fill and wash operations.

causes.cause: Blocked or disconnected pressure sensor hose
Clogged air dome at the tub connection

symptoms: E5 displayed during fill or wash cycle
Dishwasher overfills or underfills
Washer repeatedly fills and drains
Cycle does not progress past the fill stage
Inconsistent water levels between cycles

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure sensor hose
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pressure sensor, typically located in the door or side panel. Verify the small rubber hose is connected at both ends and is not kinked or cracked.

fixSteps.title: Clear the air dome
fixSteps.instruction: Locate where the pressure hose connects to the tub. Clean any food debris or mineral buildup from the air dome fitting.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and blow gently into the port. Listen for a click. If no click is heard, the sensor is faulty. Test with a multimeter for continuity change.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor and install the replacement. Reconnect the hose and wiring. Run a test cycle to verify.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and hose are fine but E5 persists, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E5 error water level sensor fix

faq.q: Can E5 cause the dishwasher to overflow?
faq.a: Yes. If the sensor cannot read the water level, the unit may overfill. The overflow protection (E4) serves as a backup safety.

faq.q: Is the pressure sensor the same as the float switch?
faq.a: No. The pressure sensor measures water level via air pressure in a hose. The float switch is a separate overflow safety device.

faq.q: How much does the sensor cost?
faq.a: The pressure sensor costs approximately $10 to $25 and is relatively easy to replace.

title: Door Switch Error
shortDescription: The door switch is not detecting the door as properly closed.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates the door switch does not detect that the dishwasher door is fully latched. The machine will not start or will pause mid-cycle until the door is confirmed closed. The door latch, switch, or wiring may be at fault.

causes.cause: Door not fully closed or latched
Wiring issue between door switch and control board

symptoms: E6 displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher will not start any cycle
Cycle pauses mid-operation
Door latch does not click when closing
Door light does not respond to opening and closing

fixSteps.title: Ensure door is fully closed
fixSteps.instruction: Push the door firmly until you hear the latch click. Check that no dishes, racks, or utensils are preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door while watching the latch mechanism. It should engage smoothly. If the latch is damaged, bent, or does not click, it needs adjustment or replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the door switch behind the inner door panel. Disconnect and test with a multimeter. The switch should show continuity when pressed and no continuity when released.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door latch or switch
fixSteps.instruction: If either component has failed, remove the inner door panel screws, disconnect the faulty part, and install the replacement. Reassemble and test.

whenToCallPro: If the door switch or latch replacement does not resolve E6 and a wiring issue is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E6 error door latch switch fix

faq.q: Can a warped door cause E6?
faq.a: Yes. If the dishwasher door or frame is warped, the latch cannot engage properly. Check alignment and adjust the door hinges if possible.

faq.q: Is E6 a safety feature?
faq.a: Yes. The door switch prevents the dishwasher from running with an open door, which would cause water to spray into the kitchen.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door switch?
faq.a: Never bypass the door switch. It is a critical safety device that prevents water damage and potential injury.

title: Wash Motor Error
shortDescription: The main wash pump motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The E7 error code indicates the main wash motor (circulation pump) has failed to start or is not running at the correct speed. Without the wash motor, water cannot be sprayed through the spray arms to clean dishes.

causes.cause: Defective wash motor or circulation pump
Wash motor jammed by food debris or broken glass

symptoms: E7 displayed during wash cycle
No water spray from the spray arms
Humming noise from the bottom of the dishwasher
Dishes come out completely dirty
Cycle starts but no washing action occurs

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly. Look for glass shards, food debris, or foreign objects that might jam the wash motor impeller. Clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the wash motor underneath. Verify all electrical connections are secure and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wires and test with a multimeter for continuity. Also test the motor capacitor if present. Replace faulty components.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wash motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor has failed, disconnect water lines and electrical connections. Remove the old motor and install the replacement. Reconnect everything and run a test cycle.

whenToCallPro: Wash motor replacement involves plumbing and electrical work. Consider professional help if you are not experienced.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E7 error wash motor pump fix

faq.q: Can glass cause E7?
faq.a: Yes. Broken glass shards can jam the wash motor impeller, preventing it from spinning. Always remove broken glass immediately.

faq.q: Is the wash motor the same as the drain pump?
faq.a: No. The wash motor circulates water through the spray arms, while the drain pump removes water from the tub. They are separate components.

faq.q: How long does a wash motor typically last?
faq.a: Wash motors typically last 8 to 12 years with normal use. Hard water and food debris can shorten the lifespan.

title: Turbidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water turbidity (clarity) sensor is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: The E8 error code indicates the turbidity sensor, which measures how dirty the wash water is, has returned abnormal readings. This sensor helps the dishwasher determine when dishes are clean and whether additional rinse cycles are needed. A faulty sensor can result in poor cleaning or excessively long cycles.

causes.cause: Dirty or coated turbidity sensor lens

symptoms: E8 displayed on the control panel
Cycles run much longer than normal
Dishes come out with residue despite long cycles
Dishwasher uses excessive water
Rinse cycle repeats multiple times

fixSteps.title: Clean the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the turbidity sensor at the bottom of the dishwasher tub, usually near the drain area. Clean the sensor lens with a soft cloth and white vinegar to remove mineral deposits and film.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the sensor wiring. Ensure the connector is secure and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor and test with a multimeter according to service manual specifications. Replace if readings are abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old sensor from its mounting. Install the new sensor in the same position and reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor replacement does not resolve E8.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E8 error turbidity sensor fix

faq.q: What is a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: It is an optical sensor that measures how cloudy or dirty the wash water is. Clear water indicates the dishes are clean.

faq.q: Can hard water affect the turbidity sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Mineral deposits from hard water can coat the sensor lens, causing inaccurate readings. Regular cleaning with vinegar helps.

faq.q: Do all Midea dishwashers have a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: Not all models. Basic models may not have this sensor. Check your model specifications.

title: Drying System Error
shortDescription: The drying system is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: The E9 error code indicates the dishwasher drying system has malfunctioned. Depending on the model, this could involve a heated drying element, a drying fan, or an auto-open door mechanism. Dishes will remain wet after the cycle completes.
symptoms: E9 displayed during or after the drying phase
Dishes and interior are still wet after the cycle
No heat felt during the drying phase
Door does not auto-open on models with this feature
Drying fan not running

fixSteps.title: Check the drying element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Access the drying element, usually located at the bottom of the tub. Test with a multimeter for continuity. Normal reading is 10 to 30 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check the drying fan
fixSteps.instruction: On models with a drying fan, locate it on the door or side panel. Verify it spins freely and test the motor with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Inspect auto-open mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: On models with auto-open drying, check the door actuator for jams or obstructions. The mechanism should push the door open slightly at the end of the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the failed drying element, fan motor, or door actuator as identified in testing.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable testing electrical components. The drying element operates at high temperatures.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E9 error drying system fix

faq.q: Can I skip the drying cycle?
faq.a: Yes. You can open the door after the wash and rinse cycles to air-dry dishes. However, the underlying issue should still be repaired.

faq.q: Why are my dishes still wet without an error code?
faq.a: Using rinse aid helps water sheet off dishes. Without rinse aid, dishes may appear wet even with a functioning dryer. Also ensure you use the correct cycle setting.

faq.q: Is the drying element the same as the wash heater?
faq.a: In some models they are the same element used for both heating wash water and drying. In other models they are separate components.

title: Detergent Dispenser Error
shortDescription: The detergent dispenser failed to open during the wash cycle.
fullDescription: The E10 error code indicates the automatic detergent dispenser did not open at the programmed time during the wash cycle. The dispenser door is operated by a solenoid or wax motor, and a failure means detergent was not released into the wash water, resulting in poor cleaning.

causes.cause: Detergent buildup jamming the dispenser door
Defective dispenser solenoid or wax motor
Dishes blocking the dispenser door from opening

symptoms: E10 displayed after a wash cycle
Detergent tablet found undissolved in the closed dispenser
Dishes come out dirty and greasy
Dispenser door does not click open when manually tested
Detergent residue caked around the dispenser

fixSteps.title: Clean the dispenser
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dispenser door manually. Clean all detergent residue from the door, hinge, and latch using hot water and a brush. Remove all caked-on buildup that may prevent the door from opening.

fixSteps.title: Check for dish obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure no dishes, pots, or utensils are positioned to block the dispenser door from swinging open during the cycle. Rearrange items if necessary.

fixSteps.title: Test the dispenser actuator
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Access the dispenser assembly from the inside of the door. Disconnect the solenoid or wax motor and test with a multimeter for continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the dispenser assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the actuator has failed, remove the inner door panel to access the dispenser. Disconnect and remove the old assembly, install the replacement, and reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the dispenser actuator needs replacement and you are not comfortable disassembling the door panel.
videoSearchQuery: Midea dishwasher E10 error detergent dispenser fix

faq.q: Can I put detergent directly in the tub instead?
faq.a: As a temporary workaround, you can place a detergent pod in the silverware basket. However, this bypasses the timed release and may not clean as effectively.

faq.q: Why does detergent cake up in the dispenser?
faq.a: Moisture from steam can cause detergent powder or pods to swell and stick. Store detergent in a dry place and do not pre-load the dispenser long before running a cycle.

faq.q: Does E10 affect the rinse cycle?
faq.a: No. E10 only affects detergent release. The rinse aid dispenser operates separately and should still function normally.
